---
title: "Feature Objects"
subtitle: "`FeatureSeries`, `EventSeries`, `TrackSeries` - time-aligned outputs."
sidebar: concepts
---

## What it is

Feature objects are immutable dataclasses for extracted stimulus features. Dense features use `FeatureSeries`, bounded events use `EventSeries`, and tracked objects use `TrackSeries`.

## Invariants

- `FeatureSeries.values` has at least two dimensions and its first axis matches `times_s`.
- `FeatureSeries.dims[0]` is `time`.
- `EventSeries.onset_s` and `offset_s` have the same shape.
- `EventSeries.offset_s` is greater than or equal to `onset_s` for every event.
- `TrackSeries.values` uses time on the first axis and track on the second axis.
- All three object types require `extractor_id` and `params_hash` in `metadata`.

## What it refuses

- Non-monotonic time arrays.
- Missing required metadata fields.
- Event labels or confidence arrays with lengths that do not match events.
- Track values whose time or track axes do not match `times_s` and `track_id`.

## What it doesn't do

These objects do not choose a storage backend, feature extractor, or fMRI model. They define the container contract those layers consume.

`Timeline` and `FeatureAlignment` sit beside these objects. They describe how rows in a feature object map to another temporal frame without changing the feature object itself.

## See also

[Extract features](../start/03-extract-features.qmd) · [Unified workflow](unified-workflow.qmd) · [Feature object schemas](../spec/feature-object-schemas.qmd) · [API reference](../reference/index.qmd)
